name: Pesto Risotto
recipeIngredient: 2 Tablespoons Pine Nuts or Chopped Walnuts
4 Cups Chicken Broth (low-sodium), homemade or canned
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Pesto:
1. In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, process basil leaves, cheese, parsley sprigs, pine nuts or chopped walnuts, and garlic until a smooth paste forms.
2. Gradually add olive oil and process until well blended. Set aside.
3. Heat Broth and Wine:
- In a 2-quart saucepan, heat the broth and wine just to boiling. Remove from heat.
4. Cook Rice:
- In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add rice and garlic; sauté, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes or until the rice begins to brown.
- Add half a cup of the hot broth mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until all the liquid is absorbed.
- Continue cooking, adding the broth mixture in increments of half a cup, until all liquid is absorbed and the rice is creamy but slightly al dente—about 20 to 25 minutes. If desired, add salt to taste.
5. Assemble:
- Pour the risotto into a serving dish.
- Spoon pesto over the risotto in a spiral pattern.
- Top with a fresh basil sprig and serve immediately. (The mixture will become sticky if left standing before serving.)
